mrp_clause_text:
ANNEXURE C: PRICING AND PAYMENT TERMS

1. Pricing

The Minimum Solar Price is equal to 0.06 USD per kilowatt-hour for the first Operating Year of the Agreement and shall escalate by 2.5% at the beginning of the second Operating Year and each anniversary thereafter.

The Maximum Solar Price is equal to 0.210 USD per kilowatt-hour for the full Term of the Agreement.

Between the two aforementioned limits, each kilowatt-hour of Energy Output from the Facility during will be priced at a 65% discount to the Grid Reference Price. The Grid Reference Price will be stated in KSH.

The following formula will be used to calculate the total amount due from the Customer to the Developer at the end of each month:

Payment = (1 − Solar Discount) × Energy Output × Grid Reference Price

or, if higher

Payment = Energy Output × Minimum Solar Price

but not exceeding

Payment = Energy Output × Maximum Solar Price
notes: Contract uses Grid Reference Price in KSH with 65% discount, but has USD-denominated floor (0.06 USD with 2.5% annual escalation) and ceiling (0.21 USD fixed). Payment is the higher of discounted grid price or floor price, but capped at ceiling price.
